frameworks.name: efficient coding
frameworks.one_line: Neural encoding adapts to past experience to allocate limited capacity

frameworks.name: Bayesian inference
frameworks.one_line: Decisions combine learned priors with current evidence under a common rule

frameworks.name: adaptive rationality
frameworks.one_line: Unified account spanning perception, cognition, and economic choice

core_question: Can a single normative framework based on efficient coding and Bayesian inference explain decision biases across perception, cognition, and economic choice?
key_assumptions: Lifelong learning shapes how information is encoded and retrieved
Local context modulates decisions made about diverse signal types
Common normative principles operate across sensory, propositional, and value domains
key_findings: Diverse decision biases share structure across perception, cognition, and economic choice
Sensory illusions, aftereffects, and history biases parallel anchoring, framing, and reference-dependent valuation
interpretations: A unified efficient-coding plus Bayesian framework can account for myriad human decision biases
Adaptive rationality offers common principles bridging perception, cognition, and economic choice
